title: Holoschoenus globifer
taxonomy: Holoschoenus globifer is a member of the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family is a part of the larger monocot clade within the plant kingdom. The order of classification for Holoschoenus globifer begins with Poales, which is a group of flowering plants with a unique arrangement of leaves and flowers. Cyperaceae is a part of the class Liliopsida, which also includes other monocots such as the grasses and orchids. The kingdom in which Holoschoenus globifer is classified is Plantae.

However, the effectiveness and safety of using Holoschoenus globifer for medicinal purposes have not been extensively scientifically studied. intro: Holoschoenus globifer, also known as Globe Sedge, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Australia. The Globe Sedge is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of the plant include sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Globe Sedge include infusions, tinctures, and salves, used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall well-being.
